MFA and Login Help Available Now

For help gaining access to your WSU account, please join our secure Zoom session by selecting the red Zoom button on the lower right side of this page.

WSU Technology Support Services
Crimson Service Desk
WSU Technology Support Services
Information Technology Services > Crimson Service Desk

Crimson Service Desk

Submit an Issue
Schedule an Appointment
WSU Knowledge Base
WSU Help Center

Video Call Crimson Service Desk

Contact Crimson Service Desk on Zoom

Crimson Service Desk is excited to offer a new and improved way to receive technical support. Our team transitioned to the Zoom Contact Center for all video-based customer support, including visual identity verification.

SELECT THE RED ZOOM BUTTON

Select the red Zoom button on the lower right side of this page to instantly join a secure Zoom session with the Crimson Service Desk team. No app installation or sign-in required.

For easy access, this button will always remain on the lower right side no matter where you are located on this page.

The Crimson Service Desk team is here to help with numerous tech questions! We offer one-on-one support through email, phone, and in-person.

Located on our website crimsonservicedesk.wsu.edu, we also have a library of hundreds of self-help articles to provide immediate answers at all hours. Visit WSU’s Knowledge Base at kb.wsu.edu to view our support articles for commonly asked questions.

WSU offices with documentation or websites referencing CougTech should update this information and point visitors to the new Crimson Service Desk at crimsonservicedesk.wsu.edu. WSU employees are also encouraged to visit our site and see all we offer.

Software and Resources

Faculty, Staff, and Students

Washington State University, through Information Technology Services, has officially partnered with several companies to provide educational pricing and discounts for individual, departmental, and personal purchases for WSU students, faculty, and staff.

Learn more about our different software and product offerings. Check back often as companies routinely update their respective educational pricing and other promotional discounts!

Didn’t find what you need here? The WSU Knowledge Base offers FAQ’s and information on a variety of other topics.

Software for specific use on the WSU network is to be removed after leaving the university.

Adobe Creative Cloud

Adobe Creative Cloud is a set of applications and services from Adobe Inc. that gives subscribers access to a collection of software used for graphic design, video editing, web development, photography, along with a set of mobile applications and also some optional cloud services.

Office 365 Software Subscription

This software subscription is available to enrolled students and active employees. For enrolled students, the subscription starts the first day of their first semester at WSU.

Enrolled students, along with active faculty and staff, can get their Office 365 software subscription here.

Azure Dev Tools for Teaching

Azure Dev Tools for Teaching is a Microsoft program to provide students with Microsoft software design, Microsoft developer tools, Cloud Computing Access and learning resources. This is available for all students and faculty. You are able to download software listed on the Azure website. Faculty, please be aware that while Azure software cannot be installed on the WSU-owned computers of individual faculty or staff, Azure software can be utilized in computer labs for lab situations to support education.


Information about Microsoft Azure Dev Tools for Teaching.


Student Login Portal for Microsoft Azure Dev Tools for Teaching.

Departmental Software

Departments can obtain software for maintaining their daily operational infrastructure on university machines through WSU’s site licensing program. For more information, please refer to Software Site Licensing.

Teaching and Research Lab Software

Departmental labs that teach how to use Microsoft products, or use Microsoft products for research, may be eligible for lab use software. Please contact Crimson Service Desk for more information and program restrictions.

CSD Tools

Distributed IT support can get access to CSD Tools. This software allows IT support to assist users with account access, password assistance and MFA issues. To request access to this tool, email the Crimson Service Desk.

Please see below for a list of computer/hardware repair stores that we are aware of in the area.

    (509) 335-2326

  • VGH Computer Services

Services

Account and Email Assistance

  • Network ID and myWSU
  • Password resets
  • Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) resets
  • Name changes
  • Friend ID (FID) issues
  • Class registration
  • Windows reinstallation

General Troubleshooting

  • Microsoft Office 365 issues
  • Wireless connectivity issues
  • VPN troubleshooting
